_winning_concept: Template-first + Edge + Cascade (Option A, v3 M4 score 0.885)

metadata.project_title: c1v Template-first Platform — v3 House of Quality

second_floor_ecs_summary._note: Main floor below scores all 27 ECs against all 7 PCs on -2..+2 scale

main_floor._convention: PC × EC; -2 = strong negative, -1 = weak negative, 0 = no effect, +1 = weak positive, +2 = strong positive

roof_nonzero_pairs.interpretation: Template hit rate directly enables skeleton-from-edge. Strongest positive pair.
Hit rate determines whether 5-min total is reachable.
Skeleton-first reduces perceived TTFB but doesn't change LLM stream start directly.
Parallel concurrency reduces effective first-LLM-text.
Parallel fan-out is the core mechanism behind 5-min total.
SR-CORNELL pass-rate correlates with reviewer acceptance (both measure quality).
Rerun consistency and retry determinism reinforce each other.
Memory recall and no-re-entry are mechanically linked.
Good info-gain selection reduces re-rank churn.
Improvement cadence depends on guardrail not blocking all changes.
Templates enforce rerun consistency because LLM specialization is bounded.
Template-loaded contexts are BIGGER than scratch prompts; squeezes EC15 token budget.
Edge skeleton has no runtime citation check; pre-linked citations mitigate but not perfectly.
Faster LLM text requires smaller contexts — competes with memory loading (EC13).
Parallel fan-out benefits from per-agent cache hits.
Uptime depends on provider redundancy (strongest reliability pair).
Low error rate reinforces retry determinism.
Higher coverage tends to push overhead up — classic roof negative; both drive PC.5 with opposite motion.
Quick time-to-confidence drives completion rate.
Stage gates enforce traceability at gate checkpoints.
Auto-improve cadence produces wins disproportionately on the speed PC (most measurable).

headline_insights: Top-2 ECs are BOTH about template infrastructure (EC1 hit rate + EC2 edge skeleton). Template-first is the single-most-levered investment.
Process / traceability ECs (EC20, EC21) sit rank 4-5 — PC.7 being lightly-weighted still produces significant imputed importance because process reinforces multiple PCs.
EC22 info-gain per question (rank 7) validates SS19 as a flagship component — it touches both PC.1 and PC.2.
Cross-session memory (EC13) edges out completion rate (EC19) — PC.4 memory is a load-bearing differentiator.
EC23 cascade re-rank latency DID NOT make the top 10 — it's a hard-constraint floor rather than a ranking-influencing knob. Confirmed separately in engineering_characteristics.json.
